query: CRT television static screen flicker old TV monitor
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / analog_degradation: CRT television static and old TV monitors

response.objective_caption: A vintage CRT television with rabbit-ear antennas sits on a dark wood floor against a plain wall. The TV screen glows with white text reading "TWILIGHT ZONE" surrounded by small static-like speckles. A shadow of the antenna is cast on the wall behind the set. The room is dimly lit, with the television screen providing the primary light source, casting a faint reflection on the floorboards.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A vintage CRT television with rabbit-ear antennas sits in a dim, oppressive room with murky, insufficient lighting that leaves most of the scene in heavy shadow. The TV screen glows with white text reading "TWILIGHT ZONE" surrounded by static-like speckles, casting a faint, isolated patch of light on the dark wood floor. The flat, shadowless wall behind absorbs almost all illumination, creating a suffocating atmosphere of dread and unease with no directional light to give the space dimension or warmth.
